title: Arks of Memory — Hold of First Arrival
description: Whispers reach you from Masond: an old terminal in the Hold of First Arrival woke last week, just for a moment. The voice it played belonged to a woman who has been dead four generations. The High Mason wants the terminal mapped before it forgets again.

objectives.description: Travel to Masond's first city above the cliffs
Recover three intact solar panels from the ark's exterior
Wake the terminal at the Hold of First Arrival
Mint the recovered recording as a Web-DTU

cross_world_unlocks.effect: the recovered DTU joins the Concordia substrate; cites back to Tunya:masond_first_city forever

title: Arks of Memory — The Curated Vault
description: The Asbir ark sits half-buried in the southern dunes. Its archive boots clean. What's missing is precisely what was deleted before launch — and the deletion log is missing too. A junior scholar in Asbir found the index of deletions. He hasn't decided who to tell. You could decide for him.

objectives.description: Reach the Asbir ark in the southern dunes
Convince the scholar to share the index of deletions
Decide what to do with the deletion index: publish, sell, give to Cree, give to Renn, destroy

branch_consequences.publish: Asbir's curated tradition collapses publicly. Reputation +20 with Sahm, Cree. Reputation -30 with Asbir. Possible Bloc weakening.
branch_consequences.sell_crime_world: Earn substantial CC. Reputation -10 across most kingdoms when traced. Crime-world fixer becomes a future contact.
branch_consequences.give_to_cree: Cree elders thank you in the only way they thank — silence and a token. Quiet pact strengthens. Cree+30 reputation.
branch_consequences.give_to_renn: Renn the Curator processes the index. He may bury it; he may publish; the choice becomes his. Asbir+15.
branch_consequences.destroy: The truth dies with you. Concord_alignment -0.05. Curated tradition holds another generation.
